title: Huffman Code Length
statement:
Given character frequencies, compute the **total encoded length** (sum of frequency × code length) using an optimal prefix-free code (Huffman coding).

**Input:** First line: n. Second line: n space-separated frequencies.
**Output:** Minimum total encoded length.

examples.explanation: Build Huffman tree: merge 5+9=14, 12+13=25, 14+25=39. Total cost = 14+25+39=78? Wait total encoded = sum of all leaf weights × depth = standard formula. Let me compute: merge 5,9→14(cost 14), merge 12,13→25(cost 25), merge 14,25→39(cost 39). Total cost=14+25+39=78.
Two chars, each gets 1 bit. Total=1+1=2.
Single character needs 0 bits (edge case — it encodes as empty string or 1 bit depending on convention). Convention: single character has code length 1. Total=10.
